steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Words in Context. The blank modifies “forms” that borrow from multiple genres to make something unconventional — the decisive clue is the contrast with traditional forms that stay recognizable “even as they evolve.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — dispersed: means spread out or scattered across space — says nothing about a form lacking defined boundaries. Does not fit the context of blended, genre-crossing writing. ✗
  • Choice B — amorphous: means lacking a fixed or definite shape. Captures exactly a form with no clear boundaries because it borrows from fables, realist narratives, prose, and poetry simultaneously. ✓
  • Choice C — neutral: means not taking sides or having no strong character — this describes a stance or tone, not the nature of a literary form. Irrelevant to the idea of blended genres. ✗
  • Choice D — customary: means traditional or conventional — the opposite of what the blank requires. The sentence explicitly contrasts these forms with traditional ones. ✗
That is the correct answer.
